system_architecture.overview: Isomera v2 separates source scenario materialization, curation, model training, benchmark execution, and publication packaging.

layers.responsibility: PostgreSQL/GML scenarios used as reproducible lineage sources.
Normalizes each scenario into graph, edge table, matrix, and metadata.
Produces supervised duplicate-pair datasets with filters and target labels.
Routes detector families and model clusters to each evaluated scenario.
Exports JSON, CSV, figures, LaTeX, PDF, ZIP, and model references.

stores.purpose: Publication/backend evidence, logs, reports, and curated scenario metadata.
Portable graphs, labels, manifests, and model routing.

scenario_api.purpose: Expose a reproducible source-to-graph transformation contract used by the UI, training code, benchmark runner, and report builder.

functions.output: directed graph normalized to SOR -> SOT -> SPEC when semantics are available
scenario-specific pickle path or post-hoc best-of selector

limits: Generic databases without a manifest or SOR/SOT/SPEC semantics cannot be normalized semantically; they require a mapping contract.
Best-of-all routing is a diagnostic selector and must be reported as post-hoc/oracle-style evidence unless the selection policy is fixed before execution.
Accuracy is retained but SF-Jaccard is the primary metric under pair-imbalance.

pipeline.details: Full lineage benchmark across SOR, SOT, and SPEC candidates with default labels merged with GenAI SPEC labels.
Edges and nodes are evaluated under the selected candidate scope.
Pairs outside the selected candidate scope are excluded from metric denominators.
Best-of-all is post-hoc diagnostic routing when enabled.
10 runs per detector family per scenario.

filters.effect: Defines the candidate-pair universe used by metrics.
